title: Rotten Dot Com
description: “Rotten.com was a haunted arcade, dispensing trauma in gumball-machine doses straight to kids with dial-up, who chewed on images never meant for their half-formed stomachs.”
hook: Rotten.com dispensed unfiltered trauma to dial-up kids who weren't ready for it—a digital haunting that shaped a generation's relationship with images.

title: Sandra Boynton’s Death Drive: “The Going to Bed Book”
description:
"We do not need to work harder when reading Boynton. Her work, especially when taken as a whole, rewards pause."

The post Sandra Boynton’s Death Drive: “The Going to Bed Book” appeared first on Public Books.
hook: Sandra Boynton's children's book isn't cute—it's a meditation on bedtime as a small death, rewarding the reader who actually pauses to look.

title: Writing Is a Way to Have Futurity
description:
Being a student of Monica Ferrell’s was a singularly influential time in my life. Immediately upon meeting her, I wanted to be like her: to enter a room with the same serious allure, the same unassuming self-possession. And when I first read her poems—fierce, sophisticated, sensual in every sense of the word—I didn’t want to […]

The post Writing Is a Way to Have Futurity appeared first on
hook: Monica Ferrell's poems are fierce and sensual enough to make you want to become someone entirely different just by reading them.

title: Slipshod Paradise
description:
This winter, for the first time in a quarter century, California was drought free—but not everyone was thrilled. On the Palos Verdes Peninsula in Los Angeles County, where some seventy-seven thousand live atop rugged coastal bluffs and steep hills, the near-biblical rains that have inundated the region in recent years pose an existential threat.



Overlooking the Pacific Ocean, the peninsula is some of the most coveted real estate in Southern California. It contains the wealthiest suburb in Los
hook: California finally got rain after twenty-five years, but the wealthy on Palos Verdes Peninsula are furious about mudslides destroying their views.

title: The joy and struggle of the movie makeover
description: A staple of films aimed at women and girls, the movie makeover is about so much more than lipstick and a fresh haircut.
hook: The movie makeover isn't about looking better—it's about a woman's inner life finally becoming visible enough for the camera to see her.
